body: {"rationale":"Strong bead overall — clear title, well-structured description with file:line root cause, numbered AC, present labels, and a cold-readable file list. Two rubric criteria are unmet: (1) AC ordering: the penultimate item (AC8) is `ddx doc audit exits 0` rather than the required `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./\u003cpaths\u003e/... green`; the go-test line is AC7, breaking the mandatory final-two pattern (`go test` → `lefthook`). (2) Deps not declared: the description names ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 as 'intersections' but never states whether they are blocking deps or not; the rubric requires an explicit statement (deps list or 'no deps') in the bead body.","score":6,"suggested_fixes":["Reorder AC so the go-test line immediately precedes lefthook: move 'ddx doc audit exits 0' to AC7, 'cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... -run TestDocAuditCommand green' to AC8, 'lefthook run pre-commit passes' to AC9.","Add an explicit deps statement to the description — e.g., 'DEPS: none (ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 are informational cross-references, not blocking dependencies)' — so the executing agent does not need to infer ordering from the intersections prose."],"waivers_applied":[]}

{"rationale":"Two rubric violations: (1) AC final-two ordering — the go-test gate is at position #7 but the rubric requires it second-to-last; the actual final two lines are 'ddx doc audit exits 0' (#8) and 'lefthook run pre-commit passes' (#9). The rubric mandates `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ...` at second-to-last. (2) Deps not declared — the description cites ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 as intersecting beads but does not classify them as blocking or non-blocking; the bead carries no deps field and no explicit 'no deps' statement. Everything else passes: title is imperative and names the subsystem; description contains PROBLEM / ROOT CAUSE with four distinct file:line anchors / PROPOSED FIX / NON-SCOPE sections; all AC items are numbered and individually verifiable; TestDocAuditCommand is named; labels carry phase:2, area:docs, area:specs, kind:doc, adr:024, and spec:FEAT-010; parent ddx-31e07fdc is set; file paths resolve within docs/helix/ in the ddx repo so a cold agent can locate files without asking.","score":6,"suggested_fixes":["Reorder AC so the go-test gate is second-to-last. Move the ddx-doc-audit check to an earlier position (e.g., #7), shift go test to #8, and keep lefthook at #9: '8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... -run \"TestDocAuditCommand\" green.' '9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.'","Add an explicit deps declaration. If ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 must land before this bead executes, list them as blocking deps. If they are informational only, add 'Deps: none' in the description body so an autonomous agent does not have to infer blocking status from the Intersections prose."],"waivers_applied":[]}

{"rationale":"Strong spec bead overall. Title is imperative and names subsystem + change. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with multiple precise file:line anchors, PROPOSED FIX, NON-SCOPE, and INTERSECTIONS. ACs are numbered, at least one names a Test* function (TestDocAuditCommand in AC 8), and the final two ACs are the go test + lefthook gate. Labels carry phase/area/kind and cross-refs (adr:024, spec:FEAT-010). Parent is set and DEPS section explicitly states none with rationale. Description is standalone with no /tmp or chat references. One deduction: AC 7 references 'ddx doc audit' as a runnable command, but that command does not appear in the CLI command overview in CLAUDE.md. If the command does not exist, AC 7 is unverifiable within the stated non-scope ('No Go implementation changes'), which could stall an autonomous agent.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["AC 7: Replace 'ddx doc audit exits 0' with a concrete verifiable check that does not depend on a potentially nonexistent command — e.g., a grep/find assertion that all referenced files contain the required vocabulary terms, or remove AC 7 if TestDocAuditCommand in AC 8 already exercises the same invariant. If 'ddx doc audit' is a real command, cite the implementing source file so the agent can verify it exists before running it."],"waivers_applied":[]}

{"rationale":"Strong spec-definition bead with clear problem/root-cause/proposed-fix structure and file:line references. Loses one point: AC items 7-9 reference `ddx doc audit` and `TestDocAuditCommand` but the bead body never establishes that this command/test exists or what it validates — a cold sub-agent cannot verify these without out-of-band knowledge. Also loses one point: the INTERSECTIONS section references ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 as informational but the relationship to this bead's scope boundary is underspecified (does this bead update those beads' linked docs, or only the four files named?). Minor: NON-SCOPE correctly excludes Go changes, but no explicit statement that manual edits to beads referenced in INTERSECTIONS are also out of scope, which could cause scope creep.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["In the description, add a sentence confirming `ddx doc audit` exists and what it checks (or replace AC 7 with a concrete file-content assertion that does not depend on an undocumented command)","Replace AC 7 with a file-content check, e.g. 'grep -q candidate-pinned docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md exits 0' so a cold agent can verify it","In INTERSECTIONS, state explicitly whether this bead edits any artifact owned by ddx-639af4ef or ddx-9228a484, or only the four named files; add them to NON-SCOPE if no edits are expected","Add to NON-SCOPE: 'No updates to .ddx/beads.jsonl entries for ddx-639af4ef or ddx-9228a484'"],"waivers_applied":[]}

Document graph is clean.
ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd	0.019s

verification_command passed: ddx doc audit && cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestDocAuditCommand"
rationale: All acceptance criteria are already satisfied by existing documentation.

AC 1-2 (FEAT-010 candidate-cycle vocabulary and sequence):
  docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md already contains the
  "Candidate-Cycle Pipeline" section defining candidate, candidate ref, cycle,
  repair cycle, read-only tool reviewer, terminal dispositions, and the
  numbered candidate-cycle sequence. Step 5 of the sequence explicitly states
  reviewers run "in the still-live worktree, before land."

AC 3 (ADR-024 read-only tool reviewer and append-only repair):
  docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md
  already states "Each reviewer runs as a read-only tool reviewer in the
  still-live attempt worktree, before land" (line 110-111) and "Repair commits
  in the worktree are also append-only — no git reset, git commit --amend,
  git squash, or git rebase against the prior candidate is permitted" (lines
  191-196).

AC 4 (TD-031 candidate-cycle events):
  docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
  Section 4 already defines all 8 required events: candidate-pinned,
  candidate-checks-failed, review-request-changes, repair-cycle-started,
  approved-land-conflict, repair-cycle-exhausted, review-manual-required,
  and final-result-landed.

AC 5 (FEAT-022 canonical/supplemental evidence):
  docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-022-prompt-evidence-assembly.md
  Requirement 2a already states "Bounded evidence bundle is canonical;
  same-worktree access is supplemental."

AC 6 (budget axes distinct from cost/rate-limit/no-progress):
  FEAT-010 "Budget axes" section already names repair_max_cycles and
  review_max_retries_per_candidate and explicitly states they "do not interact
  with the drain-level cost cap (FEAT-014), the rate-limit retry budget
  internal to a single attempt, or the no-progress counter."

AC 7-9 (doc audit, test, pre-commit):
  ddx doc audit exits 0, TestDocAuditCommand passes, lefthook run pre-commit
  exits 0 — all verified in this execution.

Conclusion: The documentation updates described in the bead's PROPOSED FIX
were already present in the base revision (f04ba4bc63bd7f137917e61ed05ecde37d8f8b7c).
No code or documentation changes are required.
